findings.title: Input acknowledgements are accepted without correlation
findings.detail: `EvInputAck` moves `DispatchBriefing` to `DispatchWorking` after only checking whether the ack duplicates `LastAckedInput`; it never requires the ack to be non-empty or equal to the current `BriefInput`. A stale, empty, or wrong-session ack can cancel `TimerInputAck` and confirm a brief/resume input that was never accepted, violating RSM-027's input-sequence correlation requirement.
findings.suggested_fix: Require `ev.InputID != "" && ev.InputID == s.BriefInput` before advancing to `DispatchWorking`; keep the timer armed or fail closed for mismatches, and add wrong/empty/stale ack tests.

findings.title: Rejected input is retried instead of failing closed
findings.detail: `EvInputRejected` routes through `dispatchBriefRetry`, treating a protocol refusal like a retryable timeout. RSM-027 requires `Rejected` input to feed the fail-closed liveness edge, so this can repeat an input the driver has already refused and delay recovery.
findings.suggested_fix: Handle `EvInputRejected` as an input failure terminal, cancelling the input timer and transitioning to `DispatchFailed` with an input rejection/undeliverable reason.

findings.title: Runexec reimplements the agent-input ack timer
findings.detail: After delivering input, the Dispatch reactor arms its own `TimerInputAck`. RSM-027 says the bounded output-or-stale window and input-ack timer belong to the agent-input seam, and the run reactor must consume ack/stale/rejected events rather than own a separate timeout.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the runexec-owned input ack deadline and consume shell-converted `agent_input_acked`, `agent_input_stale`, and rejection events from the agent-input port.

findings.title: Run-path deadlines are missing from the reactor timer vocabulary
findings.detail: `TimerKind` only models `agent_ready`, `input_ack`, and `ready_kill_reap`, while the comment leaves the commit watchdog shell-side. RSM-014 requires every run-path wall-clock select deadline, including post-agent-ready hang and commit watchdog budgets, to be represented as reactor timer events.
findings.suggested_fix: Add explicit reactor timer kinds/events for the remaining run-path deadlines and route shell deadlines through the same `Action`/`Event` timer path.

findings.title: Started critical sections still inherit submitter cancellation
findings.detail: `Submit` documents that once a critical section begins it runs to completion regardless of later submitter cancellation, but `execute` passes `j.ctx` directly to `critical`. If the per-run context is cancelled after the pre-execution gate, git/ref/worktree commands inside the exclusive section can abort mid-phase, weakening the merge queue's mutation protection.
findings.suggested_fix: After the pre-execution cancellation check, call `critical` with a cancellation-detached merge-domain context, and test that cancelling the submitter context after entry does not cancel the critical context.

findings.title: Queue shutdown can still admit a critical section
findings.detail: When the queue owner context is cancelled, both `ctx.Done()` and `q.submit` can be ready; Go `select` may receive a blocked submission and execute it after shutdown has begun. That contradicts `Submit`'s stopped-queue contract that stopped queues return `ErrQueueStopped` without running the critical section.
findings.suggested_fix: After receiving a job, re-check the owner context before execution and return `ErrQueueStopped` if shutdown has started.

findings.title: Strict FIFO depends on runtime channel sender ordering
findings.detail: The FIFO guarantee is implemented by relying on the unbuffered channel's blocked sender queue to be FIFO. RSM-015 requires FIFO among concurrent submissions, but the Go language contract does not specify fairness or sender wake order for competing sends, so the guarantee rests on runtime behavior rather than package logic.
findings.suggested_fix: Assign explicit monotonically increasing sequence numbers at `Submit` entry and have the owner execute by that sequence, or narrow the documented/spec guarantee if strict FIFO is not required.

findings.title: Mergeq package test allows git push inside the exclusion domain
findings.detail: `commitAllowlist` includes `git push`, and the fixture records `git push` while inside `Queue.Submit`. RSM-017 requires conformance tests to assert no network `git push` runs inside the merge queue exclusive section, so this package-level scaffold can bless the critical-section creep it is supposed to catch.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `git push` from the inside-domain allowlist, assert it is forbidden there, and record push only outside `Queue.Submit`.

findings.title: Run registry writes are not crash durable
findings.detail: `Write` uses `os.WriteFile` followed by `os.Rename` without fsyncing the temp file or parent directory, despite the package claiming the crew registry's atomic-write pattern. A crash after success can lose the independent-session run record, making restart adoption miss a surviving run.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the WM-026-style sequence used by `internal/crew`: create a sibling temp, write, fsync, close, rename, then fsync the parent directory.

findings.title: Run registry List silently hides bad records
findings.detail: `List` suppresses every `Load` error and returns the remaining records as if the directory were healthy. Corrupt, truncated, or unreadable `.json` records are therefore treated as absent by adoption callers, which can forget an active or dead independent run instead of failing conservative.
findings.suggested_fix: Only ignore expected TOCTOU `ErrNotFound`; for parse/read errors, return an error or a conservative filename-derived stub so recovery treats the run as unknown/protected.

findings.title: Run IDs are used as unchecked path components
findings.detail: `recordPath` joins raw `runID` into `.harmonik/runs/<runID>.json`, and the exported APIs accept strings without validating UUID/base-name shape. `List` also returns the unmarshaled payload without checking it matches the filename, so a malformed or corrupt `run_id` can make later cleanup address the wrong path.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate run IDs at every exported registry boundary and require `List` payload `run_id` to match the filename-derived ID before returning it.

findings.title: Replay harness does not acknowledge clean first-launch brief input
findings.detail: `execAction` observes `ActDeliverInput` but only counts resume prompts and never feeds a clean `EvInputAck` for normal brief input. Non-resumed clean cells can leave Dispatch in `Briefing` until the input timer fails closed, while the fault matrix accepts any terminal outcome, so a clean path can pass as a reopen instead of exercising successful dispatch completion.
findings.suggested_fix: Feed a correlated `EvInputAck` for every clean `ActDeliverInput` unless the fault case intentionally withholds it, and assert clean cells close successfully.

findings.title: Replay harness drops input correlation IDs
findings.detail: `feedStep` constructs replay events with only kind, time, and session, so synthesized `agent_ready` and `input_ack` stimuli carry empty `InputID`s. That means the replay matrix does not exercise RSM-027 input-sequence correlation or duplicate-ack handling with real IDs.
findings.suggested_fix: Mint deterministic input IDs for delivered input actions, attach them to `EvAgentReady` and matching `EvInputAck`, and assert the IDs match the delivered action.

findings.title: Fault matrix stops before post-terminal duplicate stimuli
findings.detail: The drive loop breaks as soon as the Run machine reaches Done even when `deliverableEvents` includes a duplicate or disconnect sentinel. `feedStep` also returns early once the run is no longer in flight, so terminal-position duplicate events are not actually fed through the reactors despite the advertised duplicate-position coverage.
findings.suggested_fix: Drain all calculated deliverable events and assert post-terminal stimuli produce no additional terminal actions, or explicitly exclude terminal-position duplicate/drop cases from the matrix.
